extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won the Champs-Élysées sprint on Stage 21 as POGAČAR secured overall victory in his Tour debut, having taken yellow from ROGLIČ in the penultimate-day time trial.
tactical_patterns: POGAČAR secured youngest Tour win since 1904 by delaying GC attack until penultimate-day time trial, reversing conventional stage racing wisdom.
COSNEFROY Benoît held polka dots nearly two weeks before losing it, demonstrating vulnerability of early breakaway-based KOM strategies in 2020.

rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: won Tour debut at 21, taking yellow in stage 20 ITT after deliberately waiting until penultimate day to strike
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: negative: lost 59-second lead in single penultimate-day ITT despite controlling race for weeks
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: positive: moved to podium at age 35 in 10th Tour, suggesting late-career GC form peak still possible
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: neutral: finished fourth overall after moving up in final time trial
rider_signals.MAS Enric: neutral: finished fifth overall at 6:07, consolidating position in final ITT
rider_signals.COSNEFROY Benoît: negative: held KOM jersey nearly two weeks but lost it late, exposing breakaway-dependent classification defense fragility
